// Profile

About Me

I'm a data engineer passionate about building robust, scalable data infrastructure that powers better decisions. I specialize in designing and optimizing ELT pipelines, dimensional models, and cloud-native data architectures — turning raw, complex data into reliable, production-grade solutions.

Currently pursuing my Master's in Information Technology and Management at UT Dallas (GPA 3.79), I've built data platforms for healthcare enterprises handling financial, clinical, and supply chain datasets. I thrive at the intersection of engineering rigor and analytical thinking.

Pipeline Architect Performance Optimizer Detail-Oriented Data Modeler CI/CD Practitioner Hackathon Winner 🏆

// Career

Work Experience

Data Engineer
Conifer Health Solutions
Jan 2026 – Present
  • Developed a scalable ELT data pipeline GCS-to-BigQuery healthcare ingestion from the ground up with metadata-driven validation and automated CI/CD promotion via Azure DevOps, eliminating vendor dependency.
  • Migrated 620+ Alteryx workflows to BigQuery by integrating multi-source data (IBM DB2, SQL Server, GCS) and reconstructing complex workflow logic into optimized BigQuery SQL.
  • Reverse-engineered client reporting queries to trace column-level data lineage back to source systems; redesigned data flow into a Star Schema with a conformed dimension serving 5 dashboards across multiple subject areas.
Data Engineer Intern
Tenet Healthcare
Jun 2025 – Dec 2025
  • Enhanced orchestration framework with execution-order params in Airflow DAGs, enabling 30+ procedures in parallel — 60% runtime reduction.
  • Redesigned dimensional model with hash keys + Type 1 SCD merge logic, enabling parallelized BigQuery batch loads — 70% faster.
  • Re-engineered 150+ stored procedures; automated reconciliation tests achieving 100% validation accuracy with 40% less QA effort.
  • Architected ELT workflows for financial, supply chain, and clinical datasets (PBAR, Cerner, HL7, EHRs) via Medallion Architecture + GitLab CI/CD.
Technical Support Engineer
Mphasis Limited
Aug 2022 – May 2024

  • Overhauled IT workflow documentation for a 15+ person global team, cutting operational overhead by 15%.

// Work

Projects

🤖 Agentic Insurance Claims System
September 2025 – December 2025

A multi-agent LLM pipeline (LangGraph) that takes a raw insurance claim — text + photos — and walks it through validation, fraud forensics, and settlement scoring with zero manual triage.

  • Agent 1 — Intake & Policy Validation: Parses the incoming claim text, pulls a 3-claim RAG timeline for context, validates it against policy coverage, and hands off any attached photos for forensic review.
  • Agent 2 — Photo Forensics: Runs the images through Gemini Vision, checks them against a PostgreSQL history to catch reused/duplicate photos, and verifies the image content actually matches the claim description before letting the claim proceed.
  • Agent 3 — Settlement Scoring: Once the claim clears validation and forensics, computes a settlement score with a full explainable decision trace — achieving 94% approval accuracy and 89% fraud detection precision.
  • Orchestration: Built the entire sequential agentic workflow into a Streamlit platform, automating 85% of manual review workload across 100+ monthly claims.
LangGraphLLM AgentsGemini Vision PostgreSQLRAGStreamlit
↗ View on GitHub
🏎️ Formula 1 Data Lakehouse
Dec 2025 – Apr 2026

End-to-end data lakehouse on Databricks for F1 race analytics — raw CSVs go in one end, zero-touch Tableau dashboards come out the other.

  • Ingestion: Engineered metadata-driven ingestion on Databricks — new sources are matched against a control table and auto-loaded as Delta tables, so onboarding a new feed is a single metadata row instead of new code.
  • Modeling: Refined raw data through a Medallion Architecture into a star schema — 5 dimension tables + 1 fact table with 35+ analytical measures — built for idempotent, incremental loads.
  • Automation: Orchestrated the full pipeline end-to-end via Databricks Jobs into a Tableau export notebook, delivering fully automated, zero-touch F1 performance dashboards.
DatabricksPySparkDelta Lake Star SchemaTableauMedallion Arch

// Competitions

Hackathons

🏆 March 2026
🥇 1st Place — 20+ Teams
Data Grand Prix Hackathon · EnVision UTD & BALC, UT Dallas
From Easy Credit to Selective Lending: Post-Crisis Mortgage Shift

Team Forecast Wizards analyzed how the 2008 financial crisis reshaped U.S. mortgage lending using 70M+ HMDA records (2007–2017), ~50GB of raw data.

  • Reframed narrative — why JPMorganChase survived, how California recovered first, and opportunities like Airbnb's rise from the downturn.
  • Built a scalable pipeline to ingest, clean, and aggregate 70M+ records; built institution-level and state-level risk dashboards.
  • Won 1st place unanimously — judges praised storytelling approach and strategic depth.
PythonHMDA DataTableau 50GB Big DataFinancial Analytics
🎖 April 2025
🏅 Top 3 — SEG Track
DataWhiz Hackathon · EnVision UTD, Naveen Jindal School of Management
AI-Powered Hand Injury Intelligence System

48 hours. Real injury data. Real impact. Built an AI + LLM-powered analytics system for Safety Excellence Group (SEG) to identify and prevent hand injuries in manufacturing.

  • Applied NLP and LLMs to unstructured injury narratives — extracting root cause, equipment involved, and severity level automatically.
  • Built dashboards with state-wise risk trends, machinery-specific breakdowns, and company-level risk prioritization.
  • SEG's Alex Gomez noted our team "truly understood their pain points."
NLPLLMPython TableauSafety Analytics
